chief_complaint: A 74-year-old hypertensive male presented with features of acute coronary syndrome.
history: The patient had a history of hypertension and aspirin allergy. He was planned for percutaneous transluminal coronary angioplasty (PTCA) of the right coronary artery (RCA) followed by dual antiplatelet therapy.
exam: No specific physical examination findings were reported.
labs: No specific laboratory results were reported, but the patient underwent procedures like PTCA.
correct_diagnosis: Acute Coronary Syndrome with Aspirin Hypersensitivity
differentials: Acute Coronary Syndrome with Aspirin Hypersensitivity
correct_ranking: Acute Coronary Syndrome with Aspirin Hypersensitivity
